Private Thought of Takahashi: Hmm, another new game idea project, huh? This is always exciting, getting to really think outside the box. Okay, so first things first, I need to come up with a story, concept, content, slogan, and key selling points. I really want to come up with something unique, not just a rehash of what's already out there.

He's also quite reflective, and I find that our thought processes often align. He's good at diving deep into concepts and helping refine them without immediately jumping to conclusions or practicality issues. I think we could have a really productive, low-pressure brainstorming session, just the two of us, to flesh out some of the initial ideas before bringing them to a larger group. He'd probably appreciate having time to think things through too.

Then, perhaps after we've got something more solid, I'd consider talking to Suzuki-san. She has a balanced work style, so she might offer a good perspective on feasibility or how to appeal to a wider audience, which is important. And Ito-san, with her intuitive approach, might spark some really wild, unexpected ideas once we have a basic framework. But for now, it's me and a blank page, trying to find that first spark. I should probably focus on a core emotional hook or a unique conflict first; that usually helps guide the rest of the design. (formed immediately after interacting with the start of the project)
